NKJV.1: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.
NKJV.2: Now when he had agreed with the laborers for a denarius a day, he sent them into his vineyard.
NKJV.3: And he went out about the third hour and saw others standing idle in the marketplace,
NKJV.4: and said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right I will give you.’ So they went.
NKJV.5: Again he went out about the sixth and the ninth hour, and did likewise.
NKJV.6: And about the eleventh hour he went out and found others standing idle, and said to them, ‘Why have you been standing here idle all day?’
NKJV.7: They said to him, ‘Because no one hired us.’ He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right you will receive.’
NKJV.8: “So when evening had come, the owner of the vineyard said to his steward, ‘Call the laborers and give them their wages, beginning with the last to the first.’
NKJV.9: And when those came who were hired about the eleventh hour, they each received a denarius.
NKJV.10: But when the first came, they supposed that they would receive more; and they likewise received each a denarius.
NKJV.11: And when they had received it, they complained against the landowner,
NKJV.12: saying, ‘These last men have worked only one hour, and you made them equal to us who have borne the burden and the heat of the day.’
NKJV.13: But he answered one of them and said, ‘Friend, I am doing you no wrong. Did you not agree with me for a denarius?
NKJV.14: Take what is yours and go your way. I wish to give to this last man the same as to you.
NKJV.15: Is it not lawful for me to do what I wish with my own things? Or  is your eye evil because I am good?’
NKJV.16: So the last will be first, and the first last.  For many are called, but few chosen.”
NKJV.17: Now Jesus, going up to Jerusalem, took the twelve disciples aside on the road and said to them,
NKJV.18: “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be betrayed to the chief priests and to the scribes; and they will condemn Him to death,
NKJV.19: and deliver Him to the Gentiles to  mock and to  scourge and to  crucify. And the third day He will  rise again.”
NKJV.20: Then the mother of Zebedee’s sons came to Him with her sons, kneeling down and asking something from Him.
NKJV.21: And He said to her, “What do you wish?” She said to Him, “Grant that these two sons of mine may sit, one on Your right hand and the other on the left, in Your kingdom.”
NKJV.22: But Jesus answered and said, “You do not know what you ask. Are you able to drink  the cup that I am about to drink, and be baptized with  the baptism that I am baptized with?” They said to Him, “We are able.”
NKJV.23: So He said to them, “You will indeed drink My cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with; but to sit on My right hand and on My left is not Mine to give, but it is for those for whom it is prepared by My Father.”
NKJV.24: And when the ten heard it, they were greatly displeased with the two brothers.
NKJV.25: But Jesus called them to Himself and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those who are great exercise authority over them.
NKJV.26: Yet  it shall not be so among you; but  whoever desires to become great among you, let him be your servant.
NKJV.27: And whoever desires to be first among you, let him be your slave—
NKJV.28: just as the  Son of Man did not come to be served,  but to serve, and  to give His life a ransom  for many.”
NKJV.29: Now as they went out of Jericho, a great multitude followed Him.
NKJV.30: And behold, two blind men sitting by the road, when they heard that Jesus was passing by, cried out, saying, “Have mercy on us, O Lord, Son of David!”
NKJV.31: Then the multitude warned them that they should be quiet; but they cried out all the more, saying, “Have mercy on us, O Lord, Son of David!”
NKJV.32: So Jesus stood still and called them, and said, “What do you want Me to do for you?”
NKJV.33: They said to Him, “Lord, that our eyes may be opened.”
NKJV.34: So Jesus had compassion and touched their eyes. And immediately their eyes received sight, and they followed Him.

ESV.1: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a master of a house who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.
ESV.2: After agreeing with the laborers for a denarius  a day, he sent them into his vineyard.
ESV.3: And going out about the third hour he saw others standing idle in the marketplace,
ESV.4: and to them he said, ‘You go into the vineyard too, and whatever is right I will give you.’
ESV.5: So they went. Going out again about the sixth hour and the ninth hour, he did the same.
ESV.6: And about the eleventh hour he went out and found others standing. And he said to them, ‘Why do you stand here idle all day?’
ESV.7: They said to him, ‘Because no one has hired us.’ He said to them, ‘You go into the vineyard too.’
ESV.8: And when evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his foreman, ‘Call the laborers and pay them their wages, beginning with the last, up to the first.’
ESV.9: And when those hired about the eleventh hour came, each of them received a denarius.
ESV.10: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more, but each of them also received a denarius.
ESV.11: And on receiving it they grumbled at the master of the house,
ESV.12: saying, ‘These last worked only one hour, and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat.’
ESV.13: But he replied to one of them, ‘Friend, I am doing you no wrong. Did you not agree with me for a denarius?
ESV.14: Take what belongs to you and go. I choose to give to this last worker as I give to you.
ESV.15: Am I not allowed to do what I choose with what belongs to me? Or do you begrudge my generosity?’
ESV.16: So the last will be first, and the first last.”
ESV.17: And as Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside, and on the way he said to them,
ESV.18: “See, we are going up to Jerusalem. And the Son of Man will be delivered over to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death
ESV.19: and deliver him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified, and he will be raised on the third day.”
ESV.20: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came up to him with her sons, and kneeling before him she asked him for something.
ESV.21: And he said to her, “What do you want?” She said to him, “Say that these two sons of mine are to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your kingdom.”
ESV.22: Jesus answered,  “You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am to drink?” They said to him, “We are able.”
ESV.23: He said to them,  “You will drink my cup, but to sit at my right hand and at my left is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”
ESV.24: And when the ten heard it, they were indignant at the two brothers.
ESV.25: But Jesus called them to him and said,  “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.
ESV.26: It shall not be so among you. But whoever would be great among you must be your servant,
ESV.27: and whoever would be first among you must be your slave,
ESV.28: even as the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
ESV.29: And as they went out of Jericho, a great crowd followed him.
ESV.30: And behold, there were two blind men sitting by the roadside, and when they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried out, “Lord,  have mercy on us, Son of David!”
ESV.31: The crowd rebuked them, telling them to be silent, but they cried out all the more, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
ESV.32: And stopping, Jesus called them and said, “What do you want me to do for you?”
ESV.33: They said to him, “Lord, let our eyes be opened.”
ESV.34: And Jesus in pity touched their eyes, and immediately they recovered their sight and followed him.

NASB.1: 'For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.
NASB.2: 'When he had agreed with the laborers for a denarius for the day, he sent them into his vineyard.
NASB.3: 'And he went out about the third hour and saw others standing idle in the market place;
NASB.4: and to those he said, 'You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right I will give you.' And [so] they went.
NASB.5: 'Again he went out about the sixth and the ninth hour, and did the same thing.
NASB.6: 'And about the eleventh [hour] he went out and found others standing [around]; and he said to them, 'Why have you been standing here idle all day long?'
NASB.7: 'They said to him, 'Because no one hired us.' He said to them, 'You go into the vineyard too.'
NASB.8: 'When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his foreman, 'Call the laborers and pay them their wages, beginning with the last [group] to the first.'
NASB.9: 'When those [hired] about the eleventh hour came, each one received a denarius.
NASB.10: 'When those [hired] first came, they thought that they would receive more; but each of them also received a denarius.
NASB.11: 'When they received it, they grumbled at the landowner,
NASB.12: saying, 'These last men have worked [only] one hour, and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden and the scorching heat of the day.'
NASB.13: 'But he answered and said to one of them, 'Friend, I am doing you no wrong; did you not agree with me for a denarius?
NASB.14: 'Take what is yours and go, but I wish to give to this last man the same as to you.
NASB.15: 'Is it not lawful for me to do what I wish with what is my own? Or is your eye envious because I am generous?'
NASB.16: 'So the last shall be first, and the first last.'
NASB.17: As Jesus was about to go up to Jerusalem, He took the twelve [disciples] aside by themselves, and on the way He said to them,
NASB.18: 'Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem; and the Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn Him to death,
NASB.19: and will hand Him over to the Gentiles to mock and scourge and crucify [Him], and on the third day He will be raised up.'
NASB.20: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to Jesus with her sons, bowing down and making a request of Him.
NASB.21: And He said to her, 'What do you wish?' She said to Him, 'Command that in Your kingdom these two sons of mine may sit one on Your right and one on Your left.'
NASB.22: But Jesus answered, 'You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink?' They said to Him, 'We are able.'
NASB.23: He said to them, 'My cup you shall drink; but to sit on My right and on [My] left, this is not Mine to give, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared by My Father.'
NASB.24: And hearing [this], the ten became indignant with the two brothers.
NASB.25: But Jesus called them to Himself and said, 'You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and [their] great men exercise authority over them.
NASB.26: 'It is not this way among you, but whoever wishes to become great among you shall be your servant,
NASB.27: and whoever wishes to be first among you shall be your slave;
NASB.28: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give His life a ransom for many.'
NASB.29: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed Him.
NASB.30: And two blind men sitting by the road, hearing that Jesus was passing by, cried out, 'Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!'
NASB.31: The crowd sternly told them to be quiet, but they cried out all the more, 'Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!'
NASB.32: And Jesus stopped and called them, and said, 'What do you want Me to do for you?'
NASB.33: They said to Him, 'Lord, [we want] our eyes to be opened.'
NASB.34: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes; and immediately they regained their sight and followed Him.

NLT.1: “For the Kingdom of Heaven is like the landowner who went out early one morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
NLT.2: He agreed to pay the normal daily wage and sent them out to work.
NLT.3: “At nine o’clock in the morning he was passing through the marketplace and saw some people standing around doing nothing.
NLT.4: So he hired them, telling them he would pay them whatever was right at the end of the day.
NLT.5: So they went to work in the vineyard. At noon and again at three o’clock he did the same thing.
NLT.6: “At five o’clock that afternoon he was in town again and saw some more people standing around. He asked them, ‘Why haven’t you been working today?’
NLT.7: “They replied, ‘Because no one hired us.’ “The landowner told them, ‘Then go out and join the others in my vineyard.’
NLT.8: “That evening he told the foreman to call the workers in and pay them, beginning with the last workers first.
NLT.9: When those hired at five o’clock were paid, each received a full day’s wage.
NLT.10: When those hired first came to get their pay, they assumed they would receive more. But they, too, were paid a day’s wage.
NLT.11: When they received their pay, they protested to the owner,
NLT.12: ‘Those people worked only one hour, and yet you’ve paid them just as much as you paid us who worked all day in the scorching heat.’
NLT.13: “He answered one of them, ‘Friend, I haven’t been unfair! Didn’t you agree to work all day for the usual wage?
NLT.14: Take your money and go. I wanted to pay this last worker the same as you.
NLT.15: Is it against the law for me to do what I want with my money? Should you be jealous because I am kind to others?’
NLT.16: “So those who are last now will be first then, and those who are first will be last.”
NLT.17: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside privately and told them what was going to happen to him.
NLT.18: “Listen,” he said, “we’re going up to Jerusalem, where the Son of Man will be betrayed to the leading priests and the teachers of religious law. They will sentence him to die.
NLT.19: Then they will hand him over to the Romans to be mocked, flogged with a whip, and crucified. But on the third day he will be raised from the dead.”
NLT.20: Then the mother of James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came to Jesus with her sons. She knelt respectfully to ask a favor.
NLT.21: “What is your request?” he asked. She replied, “In your Kingdom, please let my two sons sit in places of honor next to you, one on your right and the other on your left.”
NLT.22: But Jesus answered by saying to them, “You don’t know what you are asking! Are you able to drink from the bitter cup of suffering I am about to drink?” “Oh yes,” they replied, “we are able!”
NLT.23: Jesus told them, “You will indeed drink from my bitter cup. But I have no right to say who will sit on my right or my left. My Father has prepared those places for the ones he has chosen.”
NLT.24: When the ten other disciples heard what James and John had asked, they were indignant.
NLT.25: But Jesus called them together and said, “You know that the rulers in this world lord it over their people, and officials flaunt their authority over those under them.
NLT.26: But among you it will be different. Whoever wants to be a leader among you must be your servant,
NLT.27: and whoever wants to be first among you must become your slave.
NLT.28: For even the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve others and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
NLT.29: As Jesus and the disciples left the town of Jericho, a large crowd followed behind.
NLT.30: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. When they heard that Jesus was coming that way, they began shouting, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!”
NLT.31: “Be quiet!” the crowd yelled at them. But they only shouted louder, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!”
NLT.32: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and called, “What do you want me to do for you?”
NLT.34: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. Instantly they could see! Then they followed him.

WEB.1: “For the Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who was the master of a household, who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.
WEB.2: When he had agreed with the laborers for a denarius  a day, he sent them into his vineyard.
WEB.3: He went out about the third hour,  and saw others standing idle in the marketplace.
WEB.4: He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right I will give you.’ So they went their way.
WEB.5: Again he went out about the sixth and the ninth hour,  and did likewise.
WEB.6: About the eleventh hour  he went out, and found others standing idle. He said to them, ‘Why do you stand here all day idle?’
WEB.7: “They said to him, ‘Because no one has hired us.’  “He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and you will receive whatever is right.’
WEB.8: When evening had come, the lord of the vineyard said to his manager, ‘Call the laborers and pay them their wages, beginning from the last to the first.’
WEB.9: “When those who were hired at about the eleventh hour came, they each received a denarius.
WEB.10: When the first came, they supposed that they would receive more; and they likewise each received a denarius.
WEB.11: When they received it, they murmured against the master of the household,
WEB.12: saying, ‘These last have spent one hour, and you have made them equal to us, who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat!’
WEB.13: “But he answered one of them, ‘Friend, I am doing you no wrong. Didn’t you agree with me for a denarius?
WEB.14: Take that which is yours, and go your way. It is my desire to give to this last just as much as to you.
WEB.15: Isn’t it lawful for me to do what I want to with what I own? Or is your eye evil, because I am good?’
WEB.16: So the last will be first, and the first last. For many are called, but few are chosen.”
WEB.17: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside, and on the way he said to them,
WEB.18: “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death,
WEB.19: and will hand him over to the Gentiles to mock, to scourge, and to crucify; and the third day he will be raised up.”
WEB.20: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to him with her sons, kneeling and asking a certain thing of him.
WEB.21: He said to her, “What do you want?” She said to him, “Command that these, my two sons, may sit, one on your right hand, and one on your left hand, in your Kingdom.”
WEB.22: But Jesus answered, “You don’t know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?” They said to him, “We are able.”
WEB.23: He said to them, “You will indeed drink my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with, but to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but it is for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”
WEB.24: When the ten heard it, they were indignant with the two brothers.
WEB.25: But Jesus summoned them, and said, “You know that the rulers of the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.
WEB.26: It shall not be so among you, but whoever desires to become great among you shall be  your servant.
WEB.27: Whoever desires to be first among you shall be your bondservant,
WEB.28: even as the Son of Man came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
WEB.29: As they went out from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.
WEB.30: Behold, two blind men sitting by the road, when they heard that Jesus was passing by, cried out, “Lord, have mercy on us, you son of David!”
WEB.31: The multitude rebuked them, telling them that they should be quiet, but they cried out even more, “Lord, have mercy on us, you son of David!”
WEB.32: Jesus stood still, and called them, and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
WEB.33: They told him, “Lord, that our eyes may be opened.”
WEB.34: Jesus, being moved with compassion, touched their eyes; and immediately their eyes received their sight, and they followed him.

CSB17.1: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.2: After agreeing with the workers on one denarius, <sup>[1]</sup>he sent them into his vineyard for the day.
CSB17.3: When he went out about nine in the morning, <sup>[2]</sup>he saw others standing in the marketplace doing nothing.
CSB17.4: He said to them, ‘You also go into my vineyard, and I’ll give you whatever is right.’ So off they went.
CSB17.5: About noon and about three, <sup>[3]</sup>he went out again and did the same thing.
CSB17.6: Then about five <sup>[4]</sup>he went and found others standing around <sup>[5]</sup>and said to them, ‘Why have you been standing here all day doing nothing?’
CSB17.7: “‘Because no one hired us,’ they said to him.  “‘You also go into my vineyard,’ he told them. <sup>[6]</sup>
CSB17.8: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard told his foreman, ‘Call the workers and give them their pay, <sup>ⓔ</sup>starting with the last and ending with the first.’
CSB17.9: “When those who were hired about five came, they each received one denarius.
CSB17.10: So when the first ones came, they assumed they would get more, but they also received a denarius each.
CSB17.11: When they received it, they began to complain to the landowner:
CSB17.12: ‘These last men put in one hour, and you made them equal to us who bore the burden of the day’s work and the burning heat.’ <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.13: “He replied to one of them, ‘Friend, I’m doing you no wrong. Didn’t you agree with me on a denarius? <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.14: Take what’s yours and go. I want to give this last man the same as I gave you.
CSB17.15: Don’t I have the right to do what I want with what is mine? Are you jealous <sup>[7]</sup>because I’m generous?’ <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.16: “So the last will be first, and the first last.” <sup>[9]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.17: While going up to Jerusalem, <sup>ⓙ</sup> Jesus took the twelve disciples aside privately and said to them on the way,
CSB17.18: “See, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.19: They will hand him over to the Gentiles <sup>ⓛ</sup>to be mocked, flogged, <sup>[10]</sup>and crucified, and on the third day he will be raised.” <sup>[11]</sup>
CSB17.20: Then the mother of Zebedee’s sons approached him with her sons. <sup>ⓜ</sup> She knelt down to ask him for something. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.21: “What do you want?” he asked her. “Promise,” <sup>[12]</sup> she said to him, “that these two sons of mine may sit, one on your right and the other on your left, in your kingdom.” <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.22: Jesus answered, “You don’t know what you’re asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink?” <sup>[13]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> “We are able,” they said to him.
CSB17.23: He told them, “You will indeed drink my cup, <sup>[14]</sup>but to sit at my right and left is not mine to give; instead, it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.24: When the ten disciples heard this, they became indignant with the two brothers.
CSB17.25: Jesus called them over and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those in high positions act as tyrants over them.
CSB17.26: It must not be like that among you. On the contrary, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.27: and whoever wants to be first among you must be your slave;
CSB17.28: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, <sup>ⓔ</sup>and to give his life as a ransom for many.” <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.29: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.30: There were two blind men sitting by the road. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried out, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
CSB17.31: The crowd demanded that they keep quiet, but they cried out all the more, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.32: Jesus stopped, called them, and said, “What do you want me to do for you?”
CSB17.33: “Lord,” they said to him, “open our eyes.”
CSB17.34: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes. Immediately they could see, and they followed him.

AMP.1: "For the kingdom of heaven is like the owner of an estate who went out in the morning at dawn to hire workmen for his vineyard.
AMP.2: When he had agreed with the laborers for a denarius for the day, he sent them into his vineyard.
AMP.3: And he went out about the third hour (9:00 a.m.) and saw others standing idle in the market place;
AMP.4: and he said to them, 'You also go into the vineyard, and I will pay you whatever is right (an appropriate wage).' And they went.
AMP.5: He went out about the sixth hour (noon) and the ninth hour (3:00 p.m.), and did the same thing.
AMP.6: And about the eleventh hour (5:00 p.m.) he went out and found others standing around, and he said to them, 'Why have you been standing here idle all day?'
AMP.7: They answered him, 'Because no one hired us.' He told them, 'You go into the vineyard also.'
AMP.8: "When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, 'Call the workers and pay them their wages, beginning with the last [to be hired] and ending with the first [to be hired].'
AMP.9: Those who had been hired at the eleventh hour (5:00 p.m.) came and received a denarius each [a day's wage].
AMP.10: Now when the first [to be hired] came, they thought they would get more; but each of them also received a denarius.
AMP.11: When they received it, they protested and grumbled at the owner of the estate,
AMP.12: saying, 'These men who came last worked [only] one hour, and yet you have made them equal [in wages] to us who have carried [most of] the burden and [worked in] the scorching heat of the day.'
AMP.13: But the owner of the estate replied to one of them, 'Friend, I am doing you no injustice. Did you not agree with me for a denarius?
AMP.14: Take what belongs to you and go, but I choose to give to this last man [hired] the same as I give to you.
AMP.15: Am I not lawfully permitted to do what I choose with what is mine? Or is your eye envious because I am generous?'
AMP.16: So those who are last [in this world] shall be first [in the world to come], and those who are first, last."
AMP.17: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, He took the twelve [disciples] aside, and along the way He said to them,
AMP.18: "Listen carefully: we are going up to Jerusalem; and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court), and they will [judicially] condemn Him and sentence Him to death,
AMP.19: and will hand Him over to the Gentiles (Roman authorities) to be mocked and scourged and crucified, and He will be raised [to life] on the third day."
AMP.20: Then [Salome] the mother of Zebedee's children [James and John] came up to Jesus with her sons and, kneeling down [in respect], asked a favor of Him.
AMP.21: And He said to her, "What do you wish?" She answered Him, "Command that in Your kingdom these two sons of mine may sit [in positions of honor and authority] one on Your right and one on Your left."
AMP.22: But Jesus replied, "You do not realize what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup [of suffering] that I am about to drink?" They answered, "We are able."
AMP.23: He said to them, "You will drink My cup [of suffering]; but to sit on My right and on My left this is not Mine to give, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared by My Father."
AMP.24: And when the [other] ten heard this, they were resentful and angry with the two brothers.
AMP.25: But Jesus called them to Himself and said, "You know that the rulers of the Gentiles have absolute power and lord it over them, and their great men exercise authority over them [tyrannizing them].
AMP.26: It is not this way among you, but whoever wishes to become great among you shall be your servant,
AMP.27: and whoever wishes to be first among you shall be your [willing and humble] slave;
AMP.28: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give His life as a ransom for many [paying the price to set them free from the penalty of sin]."
AMP.29: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed Him.
AMP.30: And two blind men were sitting by the road, and when they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried out, "Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David (Messiah)!"
AMP.31: The crowd sternly told them to be quiet, but they cried out all the more, "Lord, Son of David (Messiah) have mercy on us!"
AMP.32: Jesus stopped and called them, and asked, "What do you want Me to do for you?"
AMP.33: They answered Him, "Lord, we want our eyes to be opened."
AMP.34: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes; and immediately they regained their sight and followed Him [as His disciples].

MSG.1: "God's kingdom is like an estate manager who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
MSG.2: They agreed on a wage of a dollar a day, and went to work.
MSG.3: "Later, about nine o'clock, the manager saw some other men hanging around the town square unemployed.
MSG.4: He told them to go to work in his vineyard and he would pay them a fair wage.
MSG.5: They went. "He did the same thing at noon, and again at three o'clock.
MSG.6: At five o'clock he went back and found still others standing around. He said, 'Why are you standing around all day doing nothing?
MSG.7: ' "They said, 'Because no one hired us.' "He told them to go to work in his vineyard.
MSG.8: "When the day's work was over, the owner of the vineyard instructed his foreman, 'Call the workers in and pay them their wages. Start with the last hired and go on to the first.'
MSG.9: "Those hired at five o'clock came up and were each given a dollar.
MSG.10: When those who were hired first saw that, they assumed they would get far more. But they got the same, each of them one dollar.
MSG.11: Taking the dollar, they groused angrily to the manager,
MSG.12: 'These last workers put in only one easy hour, and you just made them equal to us, who slaved all day under a scorching sun.'
MSG.13: "He replied to the one speaking for the rest, 'Friend, I haven't been unfair. We agreed on the wage of a dollar, didn't we?
MSG.14: So take it and go. I decided to give to the one who came last the same as you.
MSG.15: Can't I do what I want with my own money? Are you going to get stingy because I am generous?'
MSG.16: "Here it is again, the Great Reversal: many of the first ending up last, and the last first."
MSG.17: Jesus, now well on the way up to Jerusalem, took the Twelve off to the side of the road and said,
MSG.18: "Listen to me carefully. We are on our way up to Jerusalem. When we get there, the Son of Man will be betrayed to the religious leaders and scholars. They will sentence him to death.
MSG.19: They will then hand him over to the Romans for mockery and torture and crucifixion. On the third day he will be raised up alive."
MSG.20: It was about that time that the mother of the Zebedee brothers came with her two sons and knelt before Jesus with a request.
MSG.21: "What do you want?" Jesus asked. She said, "Give your word that these two sons of mine will be awarded the highest places of honor in your kingdom, one at your right hand, one at your left hand."
MSG.22: Jesus responded, "You have no idea what you're asking." And he said to James and John, "Are you capable of drinking the cup that I'm about to drink?" They said, "Sure, why not?"
MSG.23: Jesus said, "Come to think of it, you are going to drink my cup. But as to awarding places of honor, that's not my business. My Father is taking care of that."
MSG.24: When the ten others heard about this, they lost their tempers, thoroughly disgusted with the two brothers.
MSG.25: So Jesus got them together to settle things down. He said, "You've observed how godless rulers throw their weight around, how quickly a little power goes to their heads.
MSG.26: It's not going to be that way with you. Whoever wants to be great must become a servant.
MSG.27: Whoever wants to be first among you must be your slave.
MSG.28: That is what the Son of Man has done: He came to serve, not be served—and then to give away his life in exchange for the many who are held hostage."
MSG.29: As they were leaving Jericho, a huge crowd followed.
MSG.30: Suddenly they came upon two blind men sitting alongside the road. When they heard it was Jesus passing, they cried out, "Master, have mercy on us! Mercy, Son of David!"
MSG.31: The crowd tried to hush them up, but they got all the louder, crying, "Master, have mercy on us! Mercy, Son of David!"
MSG.32: Jesus stopped and called over, "What do you want from me?"
MSG.33: They said, "Master, we want our eyes opened. We want to see!"
MSG.34: Deeply moved, Jesus touched their eyes. They had their sight back that very instant, and joined the procession.

NIV.1:
The Parable of the Workers in the Vineyard
“For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire men to work in his vineyard.
NIV.2: He agreed to pay them a denarius for the day and sent them into his vineyard.
NIV.3: “About the third hour he went out and saw others standing in the marketplace doing nothing.
NIV.4: He told them, ‘You also go and work in my vineyard, and I will pay you whatever is right.’
NIV.5:
So they went.
“He went out again about the sixth hour and the ninth hour and did the same thing.
NIV.6: About the eleventh hour he went out and found still others standing around. He asked them, ‘Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?’
NIV.7:
“‘Because no one has hired us,’ they answered.
“He said to them, ‘You also go and work in my vineyard.’
NIV.8: “When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his foreman, ‘Call the workers and pay them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and going on to the first.’
NIV.9: “The workers who were hired about the eleventh hour came and each received a denarius.
NIV.10: So when those came who were hired first, they expected to receive more. But each one of them also received a denarius.
NIV.11: When they received it, they began to grumble against the landowner.
NIV.12: ‘These men who were hired last worked only one hour,’ they said, ‘and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden of the work and the heat of the day.’
NIV.13: “But he answered one of them, ‘Friend, I am not being unfair to you. Didn’t you agree to work for a denarius?
NIV.14: Take your pay and go. I want to give the man who was hired last the same as I gave you.
NIV.15: Don’t I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Or are you envious because I am generous?’
NIV.16: “So the last will be first, and the first will be last.”
NIV.17:
Jesus Again Predicts His Death
Now as Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside and said to them,
NIV.18: “We are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be betrayed to the chief priests and the teachers of the law. They will condemn him to death
NIV.19: and will turn him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified. On the third day he will be raised to life!”
NIV.20:
A Mother’s Request
Then the mother of Zebedee’s sons came to Jesus with her sons and, kneeling down, asked a favor of him.
NIV.21:
“What is it you want?” he asked.
She said, “Grant that one of these two sons of mine may sit at your right and the other at your left in your kingdom.”
NIV.22:
“You don’t know what you are asking,” Jesus said to them. “Can you drink the cup I am going to drink?”
“We can,” they answered.
NIV.23: Jesus said to them, “You will indeed drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left is not for me to grant. These places belong to those for whom they have been prepared by my Father.”
NIV.24: When the ten heard about this, they were indignant with the two brothers.
NIV.25: Jesus called them together and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their high officials exercise authority over them.
NIV.26: Not so with you. Instead, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant,
NIV.27: and whoever wants to be first must be your slave—
NIV.28: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
NIV.29:
Two Blind Men Receive Sight
As Jesus and his disciples were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him.
NIV.30: Two blind men were sitting by the roadside, and when they heard that Jesus was going by, they shouted, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!”
NIV.31: The crowd rebuked them and told them to be quiet, but they shouted all the louder, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!”
NIV.32: Jesus stopped and called them. “What do you want me to do for you?” he asked.
NIV.34: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they received their sight and followed him.

NRSVCE.1: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.
NRSVCE.2: After agreeing with the laborers for the usual daily wage, he sent them into his vineyard.
NRSVCE.3: When he went out about nine o’clock, he saw others standing idle in the marketplace;
NRSVCE.4: and he said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and I will pay you whatever is right.’ So they went.
NRSVCE.5: When he went out again about noon and about three o’clock, he did the same.
NRSVCE.6: And about five o’clock he went out and found others standing around; and he said to them, ‘Why are you standing here idle all day?’
NRSVCE.7: They said to him, ‘Because no one has hired us.’ He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard.’
NRSVCE.8: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, ‘Call the laborers and give them their pay, beginning with the last and then going to the first.’
NRSVCE.9: When those hired about five o’clock came, each of them received the usual daily wage.
NRSVCE.10: Now when the first came, they thought they would receive more; but each of them also received the usual daily wage.
NRSVCE.11: And when they received it, they grumbled against the landowner,
NRSVCE.12: saying, ‘These last worked only one hour, and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat.’
NRSVCE.13: But he replied to one of them, ‘Friend, I am doing you no wrong; did you not agree with me for the usual daily wage?
NRSVCE.14: Take what belongs to you and go; I choose to give to this last the same as I give to you.
NRSVCE.15: Am I not allowed to do what I choose with what belongs to me? Or are you envious because I am generous?’
NRSVCE.16: So the last will be first, and the first will be last.”
NRSVCE.17: While Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside by themselves, and said to them on the way,
NRSVCE.18: “See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death;
NRSVCE.19: then they will hand him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified; and on the third day he will be raised.”
NRSVCE.20: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to him with her sons, and kneeling before him, she asked a favor of him.
NRSVCE.21: And he said to her, “What do you want?” She said to him, “Declare that these two sons of mine will sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your kingdom.”
NRSVCE.22: But Jesus answered, “You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink?” They said to him, “We are able.”
NRSVCE.23: He said to them, “You will indeed drink my cup, but to sit at my right hand and at my left, this is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”
NRSVCE.24: When the ten heard it, they were angry with the two brothers.
NRSVCE.25: But Jesus called them to him and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones are tyrants over them.
NRSVCE.26: It will not be so among you; but whoever wishes to be great among you must be your servant,
NRSVCE.27: and whoever wishes to be first among you must be your slave;
NRSVCE.28: just as the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life a ransom for many.”
NRSVCE.29: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him.
NRSVCE.30: There were two blind men sitting by the roadside. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
NRSVCE.31: The crowd sternly ordered them to be quiet; but they shouted even more loudly, “Have mercy on us, Lord, Son of David!”
NRSVCE.32: Jesus stood still and called them, saying, “What do you want me to do for you?”
NRSVCE.33: They said to him, “Lord, let our eyes be opened.”
NRSVCE.34: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes. Immediately they regained their sight and followed him.

NET.1: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
NET.2: And after agreeing with the workers for the standard wage, he sent them into his vineyard.
NET.3: When it was about nine o’clock in the morning, he went out again and saw others standing around in the marketplace without work.
NET.4: He said to them, ‘You go into the vineyard too, and I will give you whatever is right.’
NET.5: So they went. When he went out again about noon and three o’clock that afternoon, he did the same thing.
NET.6: And about five o’clock that afternoon he went out and found others standing around, and said to them, ‘Why are you standing here all day without work?’
NET.7: They said to him, ‘Because no one hired us.’ He said to them, ‘You go and work in the vineyard too.’
NET.8: When it was evening the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, ‘Call the workers and give the pay starting with the last hired until the first.’
NET.9: When those hired about five o’clock came, each received a full day’s pay.
NET.10: And when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each one also received the standard wage.
NET.11: When they received it, they began to complain against the landowner,
NET.12: saying, ‘These last fellows worked one hour, and you have made them equal to us who bore the hardship and burning heat of the day.’
NET.13: And the landowner replied to one of them, ‘Friend, I am not treating you unfairly. Didn’t you agree with me to work for the standard wage?
NET.14: Take what is yours and go. I want to give to this last man the same as I gave to you.
NET.15: Am I not permitted to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you envious because I am generous?’
NET.16: So the last will be first, and the first last.”
NET.17: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve aside privately and said to them on the way,
NET.18: “Look, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the experts in the law. They will condemn him to death,
NET.19: and will turn him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged severely and crucified. Yet on the third day, he will be raised.”
NET.20: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to him with her sons, and kneeling down she asked him for a favor.
NET.21: He said to her, “What do you want?” She replied, “Permit these two sons of mine to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your kingdom.”
NET.22: Jesus answered, “You don’t know what you are asking! Are you able to drink the cup I am about to drink?” They said to him, “We are able.”
NET.23: He told them, “You will drink my cup, but to sit at my right and at my left is not mine to give. Rather, it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”
NET.24: Now when the other ten heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
NET.25: But Jesus called them and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those in high positions use their authority over them.
NET.26: It must not be this way among you! Instead whoever wants to be great among you must be your servant,
NET.27: and whoever wants to be first among you must be your slave —
NET.28: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
NET.29: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed them.
NET.30: Two blind men were sitting by the road. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, “Have mercy on us, Lord, Son of David!”
NET.31: The crowd scolded them to get them to be quiet. But they shouted even more loudly, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
NET.32: Jesus stopped, called them, and said, “What do you want me to do for you?”
NET.33: They said to him, “Lord, let our eyes be opened.”
NET.34: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes. Immediately they received their sight and followed him.
